scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Full documented Fillout REST API surface: GET /forms, GET /forms/{id} (metadata+questions), GET /forms/{id}/submissions (list), GET /forms/{id}/submissions/{submissionId} (detail), POST /forms/{id}/submissions (create), DELETE /forms/{id}/submissions/{submissionId} (delete), POST /webhook/create, POST /webhook/delete. forms remains the only Tier-1 read stream: questions and the submissions LIST still require a per-form fan_out whose id list is EITHER the configured form_id OR discovered via GET /forms depending on runtime config -- the engine's fan_out.ids_from is a static, load-time choice between config_key and request (mutually exclusive, never a runtime fallback), so this conditional cannot be expressed without silently diverging from legacy for one of the two accepted input shapes (unchanged ENGINE_GAP from the prior review; see docs.md Known limits). This pass adds the practical write surface instead: delete_submission_by_id (a plain path-parameterized delete, independent of the blocked fan_out gap since it targets one already-known submission id, not a list), and the two webhook management endpoints (create_webhook/remove_webhook), both flat-body POSTs with no fan_out dependency at all. create_submissions is NOT modeled as a write: its body is a `submissions` array of nested objects (each with its own `questions[]`/`urlParameters[]`/etc. sub-arrays), not a flat record-minus-path-fields shape the write dialect's body construction (`body_type: json` default = every record field except path_fields) can express without inventing a bespoke nested-array body-builder; see docs.md Known limits. get_submission_by_id (single-submission detail) is excluded as duplicate_of the still-blocked submissions list scope -- implementing the detail read alone, without the list, would not restore meaningful parity.

excluded.reason: questions stream blocked: ENGINE_GAP, fan_out.ids_from has no config_key-else-request fallback mode; see docs/migration/quarantine.json and docs.md Known limits
submissions LIST stream blocked: same ENGINE_GAP as questions (fan_out.ids_from config_key-else-request fallback); see docs.md Known limits
single-submission detail read is a narrower view of the still-blocked submissions list stream; implementing the detail alone without the list would not restore meaningful read parity

request body is a `submissions` array of nested objects (each with its own questions[]/urlParameters[]/scheduling[]/payments[] sub-arrays), not a flat record-minus-path_fields shape the write dialect's default body construction can express; would require a bespoke nested-array body builder the declarative dialect does not have
